label: Soothing and Substances
prompts: Are there things I reached for to help soothe or change how I felt?
What feelings, sensations, or thoughts tend to show up right before I reach for a substance to alter my feelings?
What reasons or justifications do I repetitively use to reach for a coping mechanism?
What patterns do I notice in when I use substances or other physical means to cope—time of day, stress level, environment?
Have I had less contact with a family member or friends because of a physical coping mechanism?
What physical means do I use in times of stress?
Have I noticed myself looking for reasons to justify soothing?
What feelings, sensations, or thoughts tend to show up right before I reach for something to soothe myself?
What am I hoping this soothing action will help me feel, change, soften, or escape?
What does the urge to soothe feel like in my body—tightness, heaviness, restlessness, emptiness?
What story do I tell myself about why I need a particular form of comfort in the moment?
What emotions feel hardest to sit with without turning to a soothing behavior?
What assumptions am I making about what will happen if I don’t soothe in this way?
What smaller signals tell me the urge to comfort myself is beginning?
How do I talk to myself during the urge—gentle, demanding, dismissive, urgent?
What parts of the soothing behavior feel automatic, and what parts feel intentional?
What do I notice about the difference between the urge and the action?
What do I imagine this soothing behavior is protecting me from?
What needs or longings feel connected to a physical urge?
What do I notice about my attention when the need to soothe myself appears—does it narrow, speed up, or drift?
What expectations or internal rules get activated around comforting myself?
What do I feel immediately after soothing with a preferred comfort mechanism—physically, emotionally, mentally?
How long does any sense of relief or shift last?
What parts of the experience feel comforting, and what parts feel complicated?
What alternative explanations could exist for why the urge showed up at that moment?
What influences from my past might shape how I relate to soothing behaviors now?
What influences from my current environment might be contributing?
What would I like my relationship with soothing behaviors to look like over time?
What values or goals feel connected to the moments when I want to soothe?
What do I notice about the pace or intensity of my thinking during the urge?
How do I relate to myself in the moments after the soothing passes?
